message:
fix(simd_scan): count OSC/DCS/PM/APC/SOS string bodies as ANSI bytes (ft-nk6u9)

[MEDIUM] The unified ANSI scanner under-counted `ansi_byte_count` for
every pane that emitted a C1 string-introducer sequence. The old rule
treated any byte in `0x40..=0x7E` (except `[`) immediately after ESC
as a terminator — correct for CSI final bytes and single-char C1
controls like `ESC M`, but WRONG for the five C1 introducers that
open a *string* sequence that continues until ST or BEL:

    ESC ]   OSC  (window titles, clipboard, hyperlinks, Kitty images)
    ESC P   DCS  (Sixel graphics, DECRQSS replies)
    ESC X   SOS  (Start Of String)
    ESC ^   PM   (Privacy Message)
    ESC _   APC  (Application Program Command)

Each of these landed in the `0x40..=0x7E && != '['` branch and
prematurely cleared `in_escape`. The entire string body + `ST`/`BEL`
bytes were then billed as plain text, silently skewing the ANSI
density metric that feeds compression and backpressure decisions.

## Fix

1. New `StringPhase` enum (`None | Body | SawEsc`) added as a public
   field on `OutputScanState`. `Default` is `None`, so callers who
   construct `OutputScanState::default()` are unaffected; direct
   `OutputScanState { .. }` literals need the new field (3 in-module
   test sites updated).
2. New `ansi_state_step(b, &mut in_escape, &mut string_phase) -> bool`
   helper implementing the full state machine: CSI, single-char C1,
   string-introducer intros (`] P X ^ _`), string body (terminates
   on BEL), and a two-byte ST (`ESC \`) with carry across chunks.
3. All four scan functions (memchr, memchr_with_state, scalar,
   scalar_with_state) now route bytes through the shared helper so
   the stateful and non-stateful variants stay in lockstep.
4. The stateful fast path only short-circuits when `string_phase`
   is `None` AND `in_escape` is false AND the chunk has no ESC —
   a carried-over `Body` phase now correctly forces the slow path
   because every byte of the chunk belongs to the string body.

## Tests (9 new)

* ft_nk6u9_osc_bel_terminator_counts_full_sequence
* ft_nk6u9_osc_st_terminator_counts_full_sequence
* ft_nk6u9_dcs_sequence_counts_full_body
* ft_nk6u9_pm_and_apc_sequences_count_full_body
* ft_nk6u9_osc_body_preserves_newline_count
* ft_nk6u9_osc_body_split_across_chunks — resume OSC across chunks
* ft_nk6u9_osc_st_split_between_esc_and_backslash — pin SawEsc carry
* ft_nk6u9_csi_sequence_still_terminates_on_final_byte — regression
* ft_nk6u9_single_char_c1_still_terminates — regression

## Verification

`cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features` against
a fresh `/tmp/ft-rv-p3-target` — lib compiles clean (189 pre-existing
warnings, no new ones). The lib-test surface is blocked by a
pre-existing `CompatRuntime::block_on` trait-not-in-scope error in
`workflows/handlers.rs:7263` that belongs to another pane's
in-progress migration; that is outside ft-nk6u9's scope.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
